title: Tamil Nadu Government Formation LIVE: VCK To Back Vijay, TVK To Finally Have Majority, Say Sources
content: Tamil Nadu Election Results 2026 Live Updates: There is buzz that the Dravidian arch-rivals DMK and AIADMK may come together after over 50 years to form a government and keep Vijay out of power.

title: AIADMK Says No To VCK Boss As Chief Minister In Alliance With DMK: Sources
content: The VCK secured just two out of Tamil Nadu's total 234 seats. That's less than 1% of the total assembly strength.

title: North Korea Adopts Policy For Nuclear Strike If Kim Jong Un Is Killed: Report
content: The move comes months after top Iranian leaders, including Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and several senior advisers, were assassinated by US and Israeli military operations.

title: PM Modi Touches Feet Of 97-Year-Old Makhanlal Sarkar In Bengal: Who Is He?
content: Makhan Lal Sarkar was the aide of Shyama Prasad Mookerjee and part of his last journey, said Bengal BJP chief Samik Bhattacharya.

title: How 4 Men Used ChatGPT And Design Software To Run Fake IPL Ticket Racket
content: The police said the group had earlier tried to sell fake IPL tickets outside Delhi’s Arun Jaitley Stadium.

title: Dilip Ghosh, Agnimitra Paul Among 5 In Suvendu Adhikari's Cabinet
content: The five ministers took the oath in the presence of Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Union Home Minister Amit Shah, among other top dignitaries.

title: Adani Agri Fresh To Begin Cherry Procurement In Himachal Pradesh
content: Lucknow, May 9 (IANS) Adani Agri Fresh Limited (AAFL), a wholly-owned subsidiary of Adani Enterprises Limited (AEL), has announced its entry into the stone fruit category, which includes fruits such as cherries, with plans to begin procurement in Him

title: Why Temperatures Across India Have Remained Unusually Mild In May
content: Several areas in north, east and central India have witnessed temperatures far lower than what is typical for this time of year.

title: Goa Declares 82 Lakh Square Metres As No Development Zone To Protect Hills
content: The Union Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change has approved the Goa Forest Department's proposal to declare large stretches along the Mandovi and Zuari riverbanks as eco-sensitive zones.

title: Has Mamata Banerjee Finally Conceded? Updated X Bio Drops A Big Hint
content: Mamata Banerjee updated her X bio this morning to reflect that she was the chief minister in three previous terms.

title: Opinion: Opinion | No Permanent Rivals In Politics As Vijay Turns Tamil Nadu Into 3-Way Battle
content: Back in 2006, when former Chief Minister J Jayalalithaa formed an alliance with MDMK chief Vaiko, she told waiting reporters that "there are no permanent friends or allies in politics."

title: Probe Agency Raids Punjab Minister's Premises, Bhagwant Mann Hits Out At BJP
content: The ED on Saturday carried out searches at four locations connected to Arora and his entities under the Prevention of Money Laundering Act (PMLA), 2002

title: VCK Keeps Vijay Guessing, Decision On Support To Be Announced This Evening
content: Last minute hiccups followed what appeared as a done deal last evening, with Vijay currently having just 116 MLA-elects on his side.

title: "AI-Generated": AMMK's TTV Dhinakaran Trashes Letter Of Support To Vijay
content: The TVK had reached out to the AMMK for the support of its lone MLA-elect Kamaraj S to stitch together a working coalition.

title: Video: PM Bows Down With Folded Hands To Public At Suvendu Adhikari's Oath
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi had spent considerable time campaigning in West Bengal ahead of the assembly election

title: Probe Agency Raids Premises Linked To Punjab Minister Sanjeev Arora, 2nd In A Month
content: Chandigarh, May 9 (IANS) The Enforcement Directorate (ED) on Saturday conducted searches at four premises linked to Punjab Industry Minister Sanjeev Arora and his associates under the Prevention of Money Laundering Act (PMLA) of 2002.

title: PM Modi To Visit Hyderabad Tomorrow, First Trip Since 2024 Poll Victory
content: During the visit, PM Modi will inaugurate and lay foundation stones for development projects collectively valued at around Rs 7,823 crore.

title: Big defence boost: India tests advanced Agni missile with MIRV capability
content: India has successfully test-fired an Advanced Agni missile featuring a Multiple Independently Targeted Re-Entry Vehicle (MIRV) system. This significant development, conducted off the Odisha coast, allows a single missile to strike multiple targets simultaneously, bolstering the nation's strategic defence and indigenous military modernization efforts. Defence Minister Rajnath Singh lauded the achievement as a major boost to preparedness.

title: TVK to form Tamil Nadu govt after VCK, IUML give 'unconditional support' to Vijay
content: CPI and CPM had extended their support following Congress, taking TVK’s tally from 107 to 116 by Friday night. With the VCK and IUML now on board, the total number has reached 120.

title: ‘Should be behind bars’- Ex-India cricketer erupts over Chahal’s viral vape video
content: Yuzvendra Chahal faces controversy after a video surfaced allegedly showing him vaping on a team flight. Former spinner Laxman Sivaramakrishnan criticized the lenient penalties for such violations, especially after a similar incident involving Riyan Parag. The BCCI recently reiterated its strict ban on vapes and e-cigarettes across all IPL venues and facilities.

title: After 43 yrs, US family sells electrical firm for $1.7bn; gives 540 workers $240m
content: A US-based businessman, Graham Walker, gifted his 540 employees a staggering $240 million from the $1.7 billion sale of his family's company, Fibrebond Corp. This generous act, where workers received an average of $443,000, stemmed from a condition Walker imposed before the sale to power-management giant Eaton.

title: TVK functionary attempts self-immolation as Vijay’s CM ambition faces delay
content: A 47-year-old Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) functionary, Isakkiappan, attempted self-immolation in Tirunelveli, reportedly due to frustration over his party leader Vijay's delayed Chief Ministership. Previously known for a unique penance for Vijay's electoral success, Isakkiappan's distress over TVK's political pace led to the incident. Bystanders intervened, and he is receiving intensive medical care.

title: Massive blunder! Broadcasters show India scorecard during Pakistan match
content: During the Pakistan vs. Bangladesh Test match in Dhaka, broadcasters mistakenly displayed India's scorecard instead of Pakistan's Playing XI. This significant error, which occurred after Pakistan won the toss and chose to field, quickly went viral online, sparking widespread reactions and memes among fans. The broadcasters have yet to issue an official statement regarding the incident.

title: 'You have 10 working days to voluntarily recognise unions': Google UK staff tells company
content: Google's DeepMind employees are demanding union recognition amid concerns over their AI technology's use by the US and Israeli militaries. Workers seek a commitment against developing AI weapons and surveillance tools, urging management to voluntarily recognize their unions or face legal action. The move highlights growing anxieties about AI's ethical implications and its potential for harm.

title: 'I bow to Bengal's Jana Shakti': PM Modi kowtows to Kolkata crowd as BJP forms first govt
content: Prime Minister Modi expressed profound gratitude to the people of Bengal, bowing with folded hands after the BJP's historic Assembly election victory. Alongside new chief minister Adhikari, he shared an emotional moment with 98-year-old veteran Makhanlal Sarkar, even touching his feet in a gesture of deep respect. This marks a significant shift in West Bengal's political landscape.

title: 11 years on, Jan Suraksha schemes deliver Rs 25,000 cr safety net for crores of Indians
content: Jan Suraksha programmes have paid out nearly Rs 25,160 crore in claims over a decade, with millions enrolled in life cover, accident insurance, and pension schemes. Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman highlighted the success of PMJJBY, PMSBY, and APY in providing affordable social security, especially for vulnerable sections.

title: Rs 5.5 lakh! Arjuna Awardee slams chess federation over unpaid prize money
content: Abhijeet Gupta has alleged that his Rs 5.5 lakh winner’s prize from the Odisha GM Open, held in January 2026 with a Rs 45 lakh prize pool, remains unpaid. The Arjuna Awardee criticised organisers and the All India Chess Federation for a lack of accountability, saying repeated emails went unanswered. Gupta urged authorities to protect the “dignity and trust” of Indian chess players.

title: Coinbase outage comes a day after CEO predicts AI-powered coding future
content: Coinbase experienced a service disruption due to a room overheating in an AWS datacenter, causing multiple chillers to fail. While most of Coinbase's systems remained redundant, its centralized exchange was affected. CEO Brian Armstrong stated the company will re-evaluate trade-offs to reduce outage duration in the future.

title: From dependence on imports to self-reliance - India’s defence transformation accelerates
content: India is significantly boosting defence spending and prioritizing domestic production, aiming for self-reliance and export growth. New policies like 'Make in India' and DAP 2020 are reshaping procurement, encouraging local manufacturing and foreign collaboration. Despite global supply chain challenges, indigenous production and exports are rising, signaling a strategic shift towards greater defence capability and autonomy.

title: POCSO case against Union minister Bandi Sanjay's son; he files counter FIR
content: Union minister's son, Bandi Bageerath, was booked in a POCSO case for allegedly harassing a 17-year-old girl. Simultaneously, he filed a complaint against the girl and her parents, accusing them of honey-trapping him and demanding Rs 5 crore to drop the allegations.

title: Vijay Deverakonda meets fans with wife Rashmika on birthday; sweet moment goes viral
content: Actor Vijay Deverakonda celebrated his 37th birthday with fans outside his home, joined by wife Rashmika Mandanna. The couple's affectionate interaction and cake-cutting with fans garnered significant attention online. Brother Anand Deverakonda also shared heartfelt wedding pictures, highlighting the family's joy. Vijay is currently working on upcoming films, marking a significant phase in his life and career.

title: From Dilip Ghosh to Agnimitra Paul - what BJP’s first West Bengal cabinet looks like
content: Suvendu Adhikari took oath as the new Chief Minister of West Bengal. The BJP secured a significant win in the 2026 assembly elections. Several prominent leaders attended the swearing-in ceremony. The new government includes ministers like Dilip Ghosh, Agnimitra Paul, Ashok Kirtania, Kshudiram Tudu, and Nisith Pramanik. The state saw record voter turnout.

title: Catch-22 for visa applicants as US adds 2 more questions
content: US non-immigrant visa applicants now face new questions about experiencing or fearing harm in their home countries, with affirmative answers potentially leading to denial. This change could impact future asylum claims, as applicants might be challenged on the veracity of their statements. Immigration experts suggest these questions target legal immigrants who might later seek asylum.

title: Doctor at 71? UP man takes NEET UG 2026 exam, says it’s never too late
content: A 71-year-old man from Uttar Pradesh has captured national attention by appearing for the NEET UG 2026 medical entrance exam. Ashok Bahar, a former marketing professional with LLB and MBA degrees, is pursuing his lifelong dream of becoming a doctor. His inspiring journey highlights that age is no limit to achieving one's goals.

title: Ashok Sharma reveals how Brett Lee and Dale Steyn inspired his 'dream of bowling 150'
content: Gujarat Titans' Ashok Sharma has become IPL 2026's breakout star, consistently bowling over 150 kph and even recording the tournament's fastest delivery at 154.2 kph. Inspired by legends like Brett Lee and Dale Steyn, the 23-year-old credits coach Ashish Nehra for honing his discipline and match awareness, focusing on Test-match lengths first.

title: Nandigram to writers’ buildings: The political journey of Suvendu Adhikari
content: Suvendu Adhikari has been sworn in as West Bengal's first BJP Chief Minister following a decisive election victory. Attended by top BJP leaders, the ceremony marks a significant political shift. Adhikari, a former TMC leader, was unanimously elected by BJP MLAs. His government's priorities include judicial probes and fulfilling campaign promises, with a motto of 'Talk Less Work More'.

title: Indian sailor killed after dhow catches fire near Strait of Hormuz; 17 rescued
content: A fire aboard a wooden dhow near the Strait of Hormuz resulted in the death of one Indian sailor and injuries to four others. Seventeen Indian crew members were rescued by a passing vessel. The incident occurred amidst heightened regional tensions, with recent exchanges between Iran and the United States in the vital shipping lane.

title: Krunal reacts to viral Pooran collar-grabbing incident: 'No lafda, only ...'
content: Krunal Pandya dismissed rumours of tension with Nicholas Pooran after a viral IPL 2026 video showed Pooran grabbing him by the collar during a chat. Krunal later posted, “No lafda, only love,” while Pooran replied, “Only love here.” The exchange followed an on-field spat during the Lucknow Super Giants vs Royal Challengers Bengaluru clash.

title: Supreme Court in no mood to condone Madhya Pradesh minister in Col Sofiya Qureshi case
content: The Supreme Court expressed strong disapproval of Madhya Pradesh minister Kunwar Vijay Shah's controversial remarks, questioning the state government's decision on prosecuting him. Despite the Solicitor General suggesting the minister intended praise, the bench noted a lack of repentance and the statement's unfortunate impact. The court also directed the SIT to investigate other instances of the minister's objectionable statements.

title: 'Abhi kya karu mai phir?': Rohit frustrated by female fan's selfie act - Watch
content: Rohit Sharma went viral after an awkward selfie moment with a female fan outside the Mumbai Indians bus. As the fan realised her phone had switched off, Rohit jokingly replied, “Toh abhi kya karu mai phir?” The clip sparked laughs online. Meanwhile, Harbhajan Singh praised Rohit’s recent impact and said his earlier return from injury could have changed Mumbai’s IPL season.

title: HPBOSE EMRSST 2026 result declared: How to check scorecard, see direct link
content: The Himachal Pradesh Board of School Education has announced the Eklavya Model Residential School Selection Test (EMRSST) 2026 results. Conducted on March 29, 2026, the exam saw 862 students appear out of 959 applicants. The results are now available on the HPBOSE official website, based on the final answer key.

title: Caretaker held for ‘rape’ of 3-year-old in Delhi school gets bail
content: A 57-year-old school caretaker, arrested for alleged sexual assault of a three-year-old, has been granted bail by a Dwarka court. The court noted that CCTV footage placed the accused away from the crime scene, and the child's medical examination revealed no injuries. Police had opposed bail, citing the seriousness of the Pocso Act charges.

title: Watch: PM Modi touches feet of 98-year old Makhanlal Sarkar - who is he?
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi honored 98-year-old BJP veteran Makhanlal Sarkar at the party's first West Bengal government swearing-in. Sarkar, a key grassroots leader since the BJP's inception, was recognized for his decades of service and association with the nationalist movement. His arrest in Kashmir in 1952 while with Syama Prasad Mukherjee highlights his early commitment.

title: Delhi CEO gives ‘parental perk’ to employees, offers incentive , paid leave
content: A Delhi-based CEO, Rajat Grover, has introduced a 'parental perk' offering employees a ₹10,000 reimbursement and fully paid leave for a three-day vacation with their parents. This initiative encourages employees to reciprocate the sacrifices their parents made, allowing them to fulfill long-held desires and spend quality time together.

title: Jaiswal, Shafali receive NADA notices for missed dope tests
content: Yashasvi Jaiswal and Shafali Verma received notices from India’s anti-doping agency NADA after missing scheduled dope tests, leading to their first “whereabouts failure.” The Board of Control for Cricket in India confirmed the development and said it would ensure stricter compliance, especially with cricket returning to the Olympics in 2028.

title: ‘All that will be left of you is what was in your heart': Quote of the day by Jim Carrey
content: Jim Carrey, in a 2014 commencement address, shared profound wisdom, stating that material gains are fleeting and will eventually decay. He emphasized that true value lies in the kindness and intentions held within one's heart, as these are the only things that endure beyond worldly possessions and fame.

title: Foreign investor share falls to 14-year low; DII ownership rises to steady markets
content: Foreign investors are significantly reducing their stake in Indian equities, with FII ownership hitting a multi-year low. Concurrently, domestic institutional investors, particularly mutual funds fueled by SIPs, are increasing their holdings, absorbing much of the foreign selling and reshaping market ownership.

title: BJP’s ‘sonar’ era begins in West Bengal: Suvendu Adhikari takes oath as CM– top developments
content: West Bengal ushers in a new political era as Suvendu Adhikari takes oath as the state's first BJP Chief Minister. This historic win, following an overwhelming mandate, marks the saffron party's long-fought ideological battle. Adhikari's dramatic rise, from local councillor to defeating Mamata Banerjee, culminates in this significant power shift.

title: 'This is not my first IPL': Miller frustrated after being benched by DC
content: David Miller expressed frustration at being benched for Delhi Capitals' recent IPL 2026 matches, acknowledging the team's focus on overseas player combinations. Despite missing out, the veteran batter remains committed to supporting the squad and staying prepared for any future opportunities.

title: Piyush Goyal meets LVHM, L’Oreal to push India manufacturing and exports
content: Union Minister Piyush Goyal met with luxury giants LVMH and L'Oreal to boost India's manufacturing, exports, and investments. Discussions centered on expanding production, nurturing local talent, and integrating Indian culture into global luxury supply chains. The talks also explored new avenues for India's beauty and personal care sector, leveraging L'Oreal's recent tech hub launch in Hyderabad.

title: BCCI cracks down on IPL ‘reel culture’; players and families warned
content: Commentators Reprimanded, Players & Families Warned As BCCI, Franchises Begin Crackdown On Social-Media Practices That Invite Potential Anti-Corruption Breaches

title: Two Indians in crew of hantavirus-hit ship
content: A rare hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship with two Indian crew members has sparked international attention. Experts assure no immediate concern for India, citing previous sporadic cases. The Andes strain, linked to limited human-to-human spread, requires close exposure, unlike COVID-19. India's healthcare is prepared for potential cases.

title: Trump threatens 'Project Freedom plus' if Iran deal not reached — what it means
content: US President Donald Trump indicated a potential revival of 'Project Freedom' in the Strait of Hormuz if a deal with Iran falters. This initiative, aimed at ensuring ship movement, was temporarily paused following requests from Pakistan and other nations, citing military successes and progress towards an agreement. The blockade of Iran's ports, however, will continue.

title: CBI searches 17 premises in Mumbai in connection with 3 cases against Reliance ADA Group companies
content: The total quantum of the alleged loss suffered by banks and the Life Insurance Company (LIC) of India in these cases is ₹27,337 crore

title: Prioritise public safety when releasing wild animals: Kerala SHRC
content: The commission, in a directive, notes that the Forest department has the responsibility to safeguard the lives and properties of people living near forest boundaries

title: Tamil Nadu government formation LIVE: VCK, IUML extend support to TVK; Vijay to meet Governor shortly
content: With VCK and IUML's support, the TVK-led alliance now has 121 seats in the Assembly, crossing the majority mark.

title: Which party governs which State/UT: In maps
content: Results of Assembly elections across twelve States and two UTs since January 2024 has marked a shift in the political landscape. Here is a look at the change in ruling governments in maps.

title: Tamil Nadu government formation: VCK, IUML extend unconditional support to Vijay’s TVK
content: In identically worded letters, similar to those sent by the Left parties and addressed to the Governor, Thol. Thirumavalavan and Kader Mohideen said their respective parties extended unconditional support to the TVK, under the leadership of party president C. Joseph Vijay, to form the government

title: Fadnavis asks officials to fast-track water conservation works amid El Niño concerns
content: Chief Minister directs departments to prioritise Jalyukt Shivar 2.0 projects, groundwater recharge and desilting works ahead of monsoon

title: India conducts flight trial of advanced variant of Agni missile
content: The missile was flight-tested with multiple payloads, targeted to different targets spatially distributed over a large geographical area in the Indian Ocean Region

title: ED arrests Punjab minister Sanjeev Arora in fresh PMLA case
content: The action was taken under the criminal sections of the Prevention of Money Laundering Act (PMLA), the officials said

title: No cat in treasury, Kerala in good position financially: K.N. Balagopal
content: State’s outgoing Finance Minister rebuts V.D. Satheesan’s allegation that UDF is inheriting an empty treasury; cites a cash balance of approximately ₹6,000 crore

title: ‘Value‑added cashew apple products yet to reach people’
content: ‘Although two government research institutes have developed over 50 value‑added products from cashew apple, many remain on the shelves’

title: BWSSB collects over ₹18 crore a month in penalties alone
content: BWSSB levies penalty for three kinds of violations: for not installing Rain Water Harvesting (RWH), for buildings without Occupancy Certificate and for not installing Sewage Treatment Plants (STPs).

title: CM Revanth Reddy outlines Hyderabad’s future urban vision at Parliamentary Standing Committee meeting
content: Telangana CM Revanth Reddy emphasises a multi-faceted approach to urban planning as part of the ambitious ‘Telangana Rising-2047’ Master Plan

title: Suvendu Adhikari takes oath as first BJP CM of West Bengal; five others sworn in as Ministers
content: After taking the oath of office, the CM held a meeting with Chief Secretary Dushyant Nariala, DGP Siddh Nath Gupta, and others to review the law and order situation in the State

title: Pastor sexual assault case: Commission to visit house of the victim in Andhra Pradesh
content: Police said the Pastor had regular access to the family and allegedly subjected the minor girl to repeated sexual abuse while threatening her, and attempted to suppress the issue by giving the victim abortion pills in excessive quantity

title: Firearm, ammunition seized in Manipur; one arrested
content: “The person, who is a resident of Churachandpur but an "active cadre of Kuki National Army" was apprehended from Zou Veng area on May 8,” a senior officer said

title: West Bengal Cabinet 2026: From Dilip Ghosh to Agnimitra Paul, Ministers who were sworn in along with Suvendu Adhikari
content: BJP’s new government in West Bengal inducted several fresh faces into the Cabinet. Check the list of Ministers here

title: Critically endangered gharial spotted in Assam's Kaziranga after years
content: “Once believed to have disappeared from Assam’s river systems, the critically endangered gharial has now been spotted at Kaziranga, a truly remarkable moment for wildlife conservation," says CMO

title: Congress seeks discussion in Parliament on China's 'support' to Pakistan in Operation Sindoor
content: The questions by Congress came after China, for the first time, confirmed that it provided on-site technical support to Pakistan during the four-day conflict with India last year.

title: Bengaluru fire accident: One killed, four others critical in Nagarbhavi hotel blaze
content: The blaze is suspected to have been triggered by a short circuit inside the hotel. The fire spread rapidly and soon engulfed the staff rooms located on the upper floor.

title: Second plea filed in Supreme Court challenging Governor's delay in inviting Vijay to form govt. in Tamil Nadu
content: The Governor’s refusal to invite Mr. Vijay, as the head of the single largest party to emerge after the 2026 Assembly election, is a hostile move against parliamentary democracy, the petition says

title: Udaipur: Dalit bride's humiliation rally villagers to demand police action; political parties join
content: "People forced me to dismount the mare. I felt insulted. My family members were assaulted," Ms. Meghwal told reporters

title: M.A. Baby dismisses DMK-AIADMK alliance rumours, urges Governor to invite TVK to form government
content: Speaking to the media in Madurai on Saturday, he said the people’s mandate was clearly in favour of the Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam

title: Two women claim temple marriage in Gurugram, seek protection from families
content: Both women say they faced a threat from their relatives and sought protection, according to officials

title: Karnataka GER in secondary level up by 22.46 percentage points in the past decade: NITI Aayog report
content: The GER exceeded 100% in Chandigarh (110.1%), Goa (107.5%), Dadra & Nagar Haveli and Daman & Diu (104.9%), Himachal Pradesh (102.6%), Karnataka (101.3%), and Delhi (101.1%).

title: Hyderabad’s Gachibowli IT corridor chokes under 15-lakh-tonne waste mountain
content: Nearly 15 lakh tonnes of construction and demolition waste may have accumulated in the area over the years, so much that even under ideal conditions, clearing it could take more than a year

title: Abhishek questions credibility of Bengal's electoral process, accuses ECI of being 'partisan'
content: "Throughout this entire process, we witnessed what we believe was deeply partisan conduct by several government agencies as well as the Election Commission of India," says Mr. Banerjee in a post on X

title: Palaniswami extends greetings to incoming government in Tamil Nadu
content: “I extend my heartiest congratulations to the party that will be forming the government in Tamil Nadu,” he said in an official statement

title: 126 MLAs-elect in T.N. have criminal cases pending against them; TVK tops list with 41
content: As many as 19 MLAs-elect from the TVK, 14 each from the AIADMK and the DMK, two each from the Congress and the PMK, and one each from the AMMK, the CPI, the DMDK, the IUML, and the VCK have declared that serious criminal cases were pending against them

title: VCK decides to support TVK, says top party leader
content: The VCK leader Thol Thirumavalavan had met with outgoing Chief Minister and DMK president M.K. Stalin last night

title: ‘Jhalmuri’ turns victory flavour at BJP government's maiden oath ceremony in West Bengal
content: West Bengal BJP set up 20 jhalmuri stalls across the sprawling venue in central Kolkata, while several independent vendors were also seen doing brisk business along the periphery

title: Kerala CM debate: Kharge to hold crucial talks with senior leaders today evening
content: Congress leader Rahul Gandhi is likely to meet the Kerala leaders only after a consensus emerges from the meeting chaired by Mr. Kharge

title: Over 2 lakh acres will get assured water with completion of major irrigation projects by 2027
content: As per the government’s plan, the Nagavali and Champavati rivers will be linked by 2027. This would benefit Vizianagaram city also, as it is currently depending on the Champavati River for its drinking water needs

title: Tourism and stress hormones: The troubling reality inside tiger habitats
content: A multi-reserve study by Hyderabad-based CCMB has found that tigers living near tourism roads and human activity experience higher stress levels, with core-zone tigers reacting more sharply than those in buffer areas. Researchers warn that unmanaged disturbance could affect breeding success and cub development, prompting calls for science-based tourism regulation

title: Maharashtra records highest number of 24,171 cases in crimes against children: NCRB report
content: The cases recorded concerning sexual offences against minors, economic and cyber offences, reflect that the crime in Maharashtra has increased

title: Supreme Court to hear on May 11, PIL to regulate all institutions imparting education to children below 14 years
content: "Take appropriate steps to register, recognise, supervise and monitor all institutions, imparting secular education and/or religious instruction to children up to the age of 14 years in the spirit of Article 21A read with Article 39 (f), 45 and 51-A(k)," The petition stated

title: WFI issues show-cause notice to Vinesh, declares her ineligible to compete till June 26
content: In a 15-page notice, the WFI alleged that Vinesh's conduct had caused national embarrassment, damaged the image of Indian wrestling and violated multiple rules and regulations

title: Casual sexism and ‘harmless’ jokes force women into silence at workplaces, says panel
content: Speakers at Telangana’s ‘Stand With Her’ discussion say microaggression, tokenism and gender stereotypes continue to shape emotionally unsafe environments for women at work and home

title: Two workers killed, one injured in compressor blast at Ghaziabad factory
content: "Tribhuvan was taken to Max Hospital, while Imran and Banke were admitted to Safdarjung Hospital. Imran died during treatment at Safdarjung Hospital, while Tribhuvan succumbed at Max Hospital. Banke is undergoing treatment," says Mr. Saxena

title: PM Modi takes blessings of oldest West Bengal BJP worker Makhanlal Sarkar
content: The entire Brigade Parade Grounds is decked in saffron colours and a large number of artists are performing to the tune of traditional drums

title: West Bengal oath-taking updates: Suvendu Adhikari sworn in as first BJP CM of West Bengal, five other MLAs also take oath
content: Dilip Ghosh, Agnimitra Paul, Ashok Kirtania, Kshudiram Tudu, and Nisith Pramanik took oath of office as Ministers

title: Bengaluru civic polls: Parties divided over timing
content: While former councillors say that all preliminary groundwork has been completed, they remain divided over the SEC’s decision, with some calling the move “hurried”.

title: RBI officers seek Governor's intervention to correct promotion policy
content: RBI officers across various regional offices and the central bank's headquarters in Mumbai staged protests on May 8 against recent policy changes that link promotions to vacancy availability

title: PM Modi, leaders pays homage to Tagore on Rabindra Jayanti
content: Tagore's birth anniversary is celebrated on 'Pochishe Boishakh' or the 25th of the Bengali month of Boishak, known as Rabindra Jayanti

title: EC publishes Index Cards for Assembly polls
content: According to the data, total votes polled in West Bengal were 6,38,42,843, of which 6,32,58,138 were valid votes. In Assam, the corresponding figures were 2,16,84,656 and 2,13,97,936. Tamil Nadu polled a total of 4,93,89,958 votes and 4,91,24,329 were valid while in Kerala, 2,16,30,208 votes were polled and 2,14,71,988 were valid

title: New invasive moth threatens Ladakh’s farm-based economy
content: The larvae of the Phalera cf. bucephala, commonly called the buff-tip moth, are voracious feeders, capable of stripping an entire tree bare in a few weeks

title: Tamil Nadu government formation: IUML denies reports of support to Vijay’s TVK
content: “We have been part of the DMK alliance for several decades and there is no question of leaving it. If Mr. Vijay needs support, he should approach DMK president M.K. Stalin, since he, too, has urged the Governor to take steps to expedite formation of a new government,” Kader Mohideen told The Hindu.

title: Supreme Court asks West Bengal Governor to appoint VCs to three state-aided universities
content: A dispute was going on between the governor and Banerjee over the appointment of VCs in 36 state-aided universities

title: GBA elections in Bengaluru to be held between June 14 and 24: State Election Commissioner
content: Inputs were also taken from the commissioners of the five corporations, who serve as ex-officio members, before moving ahead with the scheduling exercise.

title: Jewar MLA expresses concern over proposed high UDF and passenger charges at Noida International Airport, seeks reassessment
content: Dhirendra Singh has sent a detailed representation to the Prime Minister, Uttar Pradesh Chief Minister, Union Civil Aviation Minister and Chief Secretary of the Government of Uttar Pradesh seeking reassessment of charges

title: POCSO case booked against Minister Bandi Sanjay’s son after minor’s complaint
content: Police said the complainant, a Class 10 dropout, alleged that the incident took place during a visit to the farmhouse and that a case was registered after her family approached the police

title: Government appoints Lt Gen NS Raja Subramani (Retd) as next Chief of Defence Staff
content: Lieutenant General NS Raja Subramani is currently Military Adviser, National Security Council Secretariat from September 1, 2025

title: Government appoints Vice Admiral Krishna Swaminathan as next Chief of Naval Staff
content: The present Chief of the Naval Staff Admiral Dinesh Kumar Tripathi, PVSM, AVSM, NM will retire from service on May 31, 2026

title: Ka$h and Carry: Patel's FBI goes full happy hour
content: former agent washington digests spirited revelations from the Atlantic . he has been distributing personalized bottles of woodford reserve engraved with "Kash Patel FBI Director" he denies allegations of excessive drinking that he vehemently denies .
